description:
Model a block of three attached row houses in SketchUp, add a stair bulkhead and parapets to the center unit, and design a south-facing solar array for the center unit only using Skelion.

Building Specifications:
- The Block: Create a contiguous block of 3 attached buildings (Left, Center, Right). Total Block Footprint: 60 feet (East-West) x 45 feet (North-South). Height: 30 feet (flat roof). This means each unit is 20 feet wide x 45 feet deep.
- Target Unit (Center Building):
  - Parapets: Add a parapet wall along the Front (South) and Back (North) edges of the roof. Parapets should be 1 foot thick and 2 feet tall (extending up to 32 ft elevation).
  - Stair Bulkhead: Create a rectangular box on the roof for stair access. Size: 6 feet x 8 feet. Height: 7 feet above the roof deck. Location: Centered roughly in the middle of the roof area.

Solar Panel Configuration:
- Target: Place panels on the Center Unit's roof ONLY.
- Obstruction Avoidance: Panels must NOT overlap with the Stair Bulkhead.
- Setbacks: Maintain a visible gap between the panels and the front/rear parapets.
- Skelion Settings: Tilt: 10 degrees, Orientation: South-facing (180 azimuth), Panel type: Standard.

Save the completed model to: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\row_house_retrofit.skp
